edges.description: Defective lipid supply contributes to abnormal oligodendrocyte maturation and myelination.
edges.source: Reduced NAA-derived acetate and myelin lipid synthesis

edges.description: Myelination defects and glial stress contribute to spongiform leukodystrophy.
White-matter dysfunction disrupts early neurodevelopmental progress.
Early white-matter disease contributes to impaired motor control and hypotonia.
Diffuse white-matter disease contributes to progressive head enlargement.
CNS white-matter and visual pathway involvement contribute to visual impairment.
Progressive white-matter disease contributes to later spasticity and hypertonia.
Progressive brain involvement contributes to epilepsy over time.
White-matter disease affecting visual pathways contributes to visual and ocular manifestations.
Severe neurologic and white-matter disease contributes to impaired feeding, swallowing safety, and reflux risk.
Progressive structural brain disease is linked to abnormal neurophysiology and seizures.
Progressive tone abnormality and reduced mobility increase risk for contractures.
Orphanet lists hearing impairment among Canavan phenotypes; the mechanistic link is uncertain.
White-matter involvement of visual pathways produces impaired visual function.
Severe visual pathway and ocular involvement can manifest as blindness.
Ocular involvement includes optic atrophy in structured disease records.
Visual pathway disease can produce abnormal visual evoked potentials.
Early abnormal eye movements, including nystagmus, occur in Canavan disease.
Structured disease records include retinal pigmentation abnormalities among ocular findings.
Neurologic feeding impairment manifests as early feeding difficulty.
Feeding and swallowing impairment can coexist with reflux in severe neurologic disease.
Neuronal and cortical injury contributes to cognitive impairment.
Severe progressive brain disease contributes to impaired alertness.
Progressive neuronal and white-matter injury leads to loss of acquired skills.

nodes.description: AAV-mediated ASPA gene replacement is an investigational disease-modifying strategy intended to restore aspartoacylase expression. Early human reports and active trials support biological plausibility, but it remains a developing therapy rather than established standard care.

evidence.reference_title: Adeno-associated virus-mediated gene therapy in a patient with Canavan disease using dual routes of administration and immune modulation.
evidence.snippet: NAA level was reduced at 3 months

evidence.reference_title: Update on leukodystrophies and developing trials.
evidence.snippet: gene therapy is emerging as a potential treatment avenue

nodes.description: Many disease-linked ASPA missense variants destabilize the aspartoacylase protein, promoting misfolding and proteasomal degradation and thereby reducing active enzyme abundance.

evidence.reference_title: Cellular and molecular mechanisms of aspartoacylase and its role in Canavan disease.
evidence.snippet: most disease linked ASPA gene variants lead to a structural destabilization

nodes.description: Severe Canavan neurologic impairment commonly affects feeding, nutrition, and oral intake safety. Feeding therapy is recommended to maintain weight gain and reduce aspiration risk, and natural history scoring treats aspiration, inadequate weight gain, or tube feeding as feeding-severity markers.

nodes.description: Standard care is supportive and multidisciplinary, including neurology for seizures and spasticity, rehabilitation therapies for mobility and contracture prevention, feeding therapy and nutritional support, respiratory monitoring, educational supports, and palliative or respite care as needed.

evidence.snippet: Multidisciplinary care by specialists in neurology

nodes.description: Aspartoacylase deficiency causes N-acetyl-L-aspartate to accumulate in the brain, urine, blood, cerebrospinal fluid, and other body fluids. Elevated urinary NAA and brain NAA by proton magnetic resonance spectroscopy are key diagnostic biochemical markers.
nodes.id: NAA accumulation in CNS and body fluids

nodes.description: Lowering NAA synthesis through NAT8L suppression is a preclinical strategy that targets the toxic upstream substrate burden rather than replacing ASPA. It is included as a model-supported investigational mechanism, not as an established human treatment.

evidence.reference_title: Suppressing N-Acetyl-l-Aspartate Synthesis Prevents Loss of Neurons in a Murine Model of Canavan Leukodystrophy.
evidence.snippet: prevents neuron loss and cerebral cortical atrophy

nodes.description: Aspartoacylase-deficient model data show cerebral cortical and cerebellar neuron depletion and progressive cortical thinning, suggesting that excess NAA and leukodystrophy are linked to neuronal injury as well as myelin pathology.

evidence.reference_title: Suppressing N-Acetyl-l-Aspartate Synthesis Prevents Loss of Neurons in a Murine Model of Canavan Leukodystrophy.
evidence.snippet: cerebral cortical and cerebellar neurons are decreased

nodes.description: Canavan white-matter disease can affect visual pathways, while structured disease records also report ocular findings. This branch connects the leukodystrophy mechanism to impaired visual tracking, blindness, optic atrophy, abnormal visual evoked potentials, nystagmus, and retinal pigmentation findings.
